commands.author: zhiqiang26
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=67ff206077b363dc68112fe30fdfe08379aad1d9)
commands.review_summary:
The PR expands Skill Workshop update/revise/apply support from workspace skills to shared OpenClaw-managed skills, and updates the agent prompt, CLI copy, docs, and tests for that wider writable surface.

PR surface: Source +33, Tests +91, Docs +2. Total +126 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level: with `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` set and no `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, existing global skills resolve through `CONFIG_DIR/skills` while the PR's Workshop path resolves through `resolveStateDir()/skills`. I did not run the command path because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The PR needs contributor-supplied real behavior proof and a code revision for managed-root parity before maintainer re-review.

target.title: Support managed Skill Workshop updates
target.body:
## Summary

- allow Skill Workshop update/revise/apply flows for writable `openclaw-managed` skills under the user's OpenClaw skills directory
- keep workspace skill support intact while preserving boundary checks for targets outside workspace/managed skills
- update agent prompt, CLI copy, and docs from workspace-only language to writable user-owned skills

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Skill Workshop previously rejected shared `openclaw-managed` skills even when they lived in the user's writable `~/.openclaw/skills` directory, forcing approved internal skill changes back to manual governed edits.
- Real environment tested: Moeed's local OpenClaw installation on macOS, running the installed Skill Workshop path with shared managed skills under `~/.openclaw/skills`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Applied the installed-runtime equivalent of this source patch, then used Skill Workshop to update and apply shared managed skills (`skill-workshop`, `skill-creator`, `governance`, `improve`, and `scripting`), inspected the applied proposal, and checked live skill visibility with `openclaw skills info skill-workshop --agent openclaw`.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output copied from the live OpenClaw setup:

```text
$ skill_workshop list status=applied query=managed limit=5
- improve-20260613-3fc2318e10 [applied, update, clean] improve: Update improve
- governance-20260613-e5af96043e [applied, update, clean] governance: Update governance
- skill-creator-20260613-fad38556bb [applied, update, clean] skill-creator: Update skill-creator
- skill-workshop-20260613-0ba4a074dc [applied, update, clean] skill-workshop: Update skill-workshop
- scripting-20260613-8e14e11f68 [applied, update, clean] scripting: Update scripting

$ skill_workshop inspect proposal_id=skill-workshop-20260613-0ba4a074dc
Proposal: skill-workshop-20260613-0ba4a074dc
Status: applied
Kind: update
Skill: skill-workshop
Version: v1
Scan: clean

$ openclaw skills info skill-workshop --agent openclaw
skill-workshop ✓ Ready

Skill Workshop lifecycle discipline.

Details:
  Source: openclaw-managed
  Path: ~/.openclaw/skills/skill-workshop/SKILL.md
  Visible to model: yes
  Available as command: yes
```

- Observed result after fix: Shared managed skill updates no longer stop at `openclaw-managed` as read-only; approved updates can be proposed, scanned clean, applied, inspected as `applied`, and read back as live managed skills.
- What was not tested: Upstream release installation after merge; the current proof is from the locally patched installed runtime plus this source branch's focused unit/type/lint/doc checks.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The live environment contains the same behaviour as a local installed-runtime hotfix while this PR carries the source change upstream; the hotfix remains tracked locally until an upstream release includes equivalent behaviour.

## Tests

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/skills/workshop/service.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `pnpm lint:core`
- `pnpm format:docs:check`
- `git diff --check`
target.author: moeedahmed
